steps.title: Identify the grammatical structure at the blank
steps.content: The blank requires occupational descriptions for Fu Pei-mei, positioned between the adjective “renowned” and the proper name “Fu Pei-mei” itself.

steps.title: Apply the punctuation rule for descriptive phrases before names
steps.content: In standard English, no comma separates a descriptive phrase from a proper name it identifies—think “renowned chef Julia Child,” not “renowned chef, Julia Child.” Choice B (“chef and culinary instructor”) correctly follows this pattern. The other three options each add unnecessary commas: some before the name (A, D) and some within the compound before “and” (C), violating the standard rule. That matches choice B — B is correct.
